response.justification: The candidate is dramatically overqualified for an entry-level Java role, currently working as a Staff Software Engineer at Stripe with 15+ years of experience. While the CS degree, agile/TDD discipline, and some Java exposure technically meet the bar, two factors make this a poor fit: German is listed as only 'Basic' (the role requires strong verbal and written German for client-facing insurance work), and Java is a peripheral skill behind TypeScript and DLang. Expected compensation, retention risk, and language gap make this hire unlikely to succeed.
strengths: BSc in Computer Science plus 15+ years of professional software development
Deep agile, TDD, CI/CD and software craftsmanship experience
Some Java background (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling at Altom)
concerns: Severely overqualified for a junior IC role — high attrition and salary-mismatch risk
German listed as only 'Basic'; client work in German insurance sector requires fluency
Java is not a primary stack — career is centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang

key_factors.factor: Insufficient German for client-facing insurance work
Strong CS fundamentals and agile/TDD track record
